title: Centre raises export duties on diesel, aviation turbine fuel
content:
The government on Saturday (April 11, 2026) hiked export duty, or windfall tax, on diesel to ₹55.5 per litre and on aviation fuel ATF to ₹42 a litre.
The duty hikes would be applicable with immediate effect, the Finance Ministry said in a notification.
Published - April 12, 2026 07:27 am IST

title: Orbicular’s Ozempic generic gets U.S. FDA tentative nod
content:
A generic version of Ozempic (semaglutide injection) developed by Hyderabad-based Orbicular Pharmaceutical Technologies in partnership with Florida-headquartered Apotex Corp. has received tentative approval from the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA).
The product will be marketed and commercialised in the U.S. by Apotex, which is the ANDA applicant, complex peptide-based generics developer Orbicular said in a release.
Published - April 11, 2026 09:52 pm IST

title: Home Secretary appoints Durai Kumar as DVAC in-charge following Election Commission reshuffle
content:
Soon after the Election Commission of India (EC) on Saturday stripped Director-General of Police (DGP) Sandeep Mittal of the post of Director, Vigilance and Anti-Corruption (V&AC), the Tamil Nadu Head of Police Force (HoPF) and DGP Sandeep Rai Rathore issued an order asking Mr. Mittal to hold “full additional charge as Director, V&AC, until further orders”.
However, the order was reversed by Additional Chief Secretary K. Manivasan, who had earlier in the day taken charge as Tamil Nadu Home Secretary, after the EC transferred incumbent Dheeraj Kumar. The Home Secretary, instead, ordered the appointment of IPS officer A.T. Durai Kumar as Director in-charge, V&AC, till a regular posting order is issued.
Published - April 12, 2026 01:28 am IST

title: Tamil Nadu voter deleted from electoral roll reminds Supreme Court of promise to protect in case of ‘mass exclusion’
content:
The Supreme Court on Friday (April 10, 2026) refused to intervene for a woman whose name was deleted from the Tamil Nadu electoral roll in the Special Intensive Revision (SIR) even as she reminded the court, as a parting shot, of its promise to step in if the SIR leads to “mass exclusions” across the country.
The petitioner, C. Geetha, said her name had been a mainstay in the State’s electoral roll over the years. She was even intending to contest as an independent candidate in the 51-Uthangarai (SC) Assembly Constituency and 52-Bargur Assembly Constituency in the Tamil Nadu Assembly elections in 2026.
Published - April 10, 2026 10:28 pm IST
